title: Divide Intervals Into Minimum Number of Groups

testcases.input: intervals = [[5,10],[6,8],[1,5],[2,3],[1,10]] Output: 3 Explanation: We can divide the intervals into the following groups: - Group 1: [1, 5], [6, 8]. - Group 2: [2, 3], [5, 10]. - Group 3: [1, 10]. It can be proven that it is not possible to divide the intervals into fewer than 3 groups.
testcases.expectedOutput: 3 Explanation: We can divide the intervals into the following groups: - Group 1: [1, 5], [6, 8]. - Group 2: [2, 3], [5, 10]. - Group 3: [1, 10]. It can be proven that it is not possible to divide the intervals into fewer than 3 groups.

testcases.input: intervals = [[1,3],[5,6],[8,10],[11,13]] Output: 1 Explanation: None of the intervals overlap, so we can put all of them in one group.
testcases.expectedOutput: 1 Explanation: None of the intervals overlap, so we can put all of them in one group.
